Core finding: no genuine English-medium bachelor or master track exists in Law, Business or Computer Science, the three fields the assignment named. Directly confirmed via FreeUni own official programme-specification PDFs (pdftotext-extracted, Georgian language): Building Systems Engineering and International Relations bachelor programmes both carry an explicit "language of instruction: Georgian" field; the MBA and Business Law/Tax LL.M. curriculum PDFs are written entirely in Georgian course titles; the Law bachelor page only offers legal-English as one subject, not the medium; the Business Law LL.M. admission page lists English B1 as merely "preferably" required, not mandatory. All five checked flagship programme pages (Law bachelor, Business bachelor, MBA, Business Law LL.M., Finance masters) route admission through Georgia own national exam system (naec.ge Unified National/Masters Exam) plus a FreeUni interview, with no separate international/foreign-citizen admission route, English cohort, or foreign-citizen tuition tier published anywhere on the official site (unlike Georgian Technical University, already published, which explicitly tags a far-abroad-citizens fee tier). A general Georgian government mechanism does let foreign citizens enrol without the exam (MES Order N19/N 2011, via NCEQE), and this exact clause appears in FreeUni own programme PDFs, but the underlying instruction is still Georgian and no FreeUni-specific foreign-citizen process/fee is published to source an offering from; eqe.ge and mes.gov.ge were both unreachable by direct curl (TLS handshake failures) so this general policy is WebSearch-sourced only, disclosed as such. A third-party admissionoffice.ge/freeuni page was fetched and explicitly rejected as an unreliable mislabeled template (its own form copy refers to a different institution, "The University of Georgia - Tbilisi", and its declaration text names yet a third institution, "Alte University"). No taxonomy-gap row logged (blocking issue is medium of instruction, not taxonomy coverage). Secondary pipeline note (not fixed, scripts/ off-limits per instructions): publish-catalog-payload.ts courseSchema.stream only allows engineering/business, so a genuine English-medium Law programme could not be published through this script even if found. Deviation disclosed: one background WebSearch sub-agent was mistakenly spawned early in this run despite the do-not-spawn-sub-agents instruction; caught immediately, TaskStop attempted (failed, task not owned by this session), its output used only as a source-URL hint, every fact independently re-verified via direct curl/WebSearch/pdftotext before being used.
